description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular shape and a wood-textured exterior casing. The front features a large, convex glass screen bordered by a thick, cream-colored bezel, alongside a vertical panel with two prominent rotary dials and a pair of push buttons, all set in a black frame. The top and sides of the television are finished in a brown woodgrain pattern, with the right side displaying a couple of colorful stickers and a visible scratch, adding to its aged, urban character. The back panel is matte black with ventilation slats, a red label, and various ports and slots, typical of older CRT televisions. The left side is plain, showing only the woodgrain finish. This television would likely be found discarded or repurposed in an urban environment, such as an alley, garage, or sidewalk.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a woodgrain exterior, large convex screen, and rotary dials, featuring stickers and signs of wear.
description_view_0: The back view shows a matte black panel with ventilation slats, a red label, and various ports and slots.
description_view_1: The left side view displays a plain brown woodgrain surface with a visible scratch and no additional features.
description_view_2: The front view highlights the large convex screen, cream bezel, two rotary dials, and push buttons in a black panel.
description_view_3: The right side view reveals the woodgrain finish with two colorful stickers and a patch of tape, adding character.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, cream-colored, plastic bezel with rounded corners and a retro style
attribute_control_button_layout: vertical cluster on the front right panel, including two rotary dials and two push buttons
